[201:45]very interesting.

[250:49]disparage him. So why are we still trying to salvage by saying, oh, it's not really Trump's fault.

[250:57]Of course, it's Trump's fault. This is his letter. I'm going to read it in full. This is from his

[251:04]Twitter account, Joe Kent. That says quote, after much reflection, I've decided to resign from my

[251:11]position as director of the National Counterterrorism Center effective today. I cannot in good conscience

[251:17]support the ongoing war in Iran. Iran posed no imminent threat to our nation and it is clear that

[251:22]we started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby. I support the values

[251:29]in the foreign policies that you campaigned on in 2016, 2020 and 2024, which you enacted in your

[251:35]first term until June of 2025, you understood that the wars in the Middle East were a trap

[251:41]that robbed America of the precious lives of our patriots and depleted the wealth and prosperity

[251:46]of our nation. In your first administration, you understood better than any modern president,

[251:51]how to decisively apply military power without getting us drawn into never ending wars. You demonstrated

[251:58]this by killing Cosm Suleimani and by defeating ISIS. Early in this administration, high ranking

[252:03]Israeli officials and influential members of the American media deployed a misinformation

[252:09]campaign that wholly undermined your America first platform and so pro-war sentiments to encourage

[252:15]a war with Iran. This echo chamber was used to deceive you into believing that Iran posed an

[252:21]imminent threat to the United States and that you should strike now and that there was a clear path

[252:26]to a swift victory. This was a lie and is the same tactic the Israelis used to draw us into the

[252:33]disastrous Iraq war that cost us our nation the lives of thousands of our best men and women.

[252:40]We cannot make this mistake again as a veteran who deployed to combat 11 times and as a gold star

[252:46]husband who lost my beloved wife Shannon in a war manufactured by Israel, I cannot support sending

[252:52]the next generation to fight and die in a war that serve no benefit to the American people

[252:57]nor justifies the cost of American lives. I pray you will reflect upon what we are doing in

[253:03]Iran, who we are doing it for. The time for bold action is now you can reverse course and chart

[253:08]a new path for our nation or you can allow us to slip further toward decline in chaos. You hold

[253:13]the cards. It was an honor to serve in your administration and to serve our great nation.

[253:19]So this is a statement and let's just say at the outset, this is a great statement.

[253:26]This is a very good and positive development. Finally, someone in the administration is

[253:33]dissenting publicly and even better has resigned in protest and has written this letter

[253:40]and Joe can hit the nail on the head. It's all true. It's all true. Trump campaigned on America

[253:46]first three times and we're not getting it in 2025 and he's right that the Israelis and the

[253:54]Israel lobby in America have hyped up a war with Iran and they have deceived the public and they
